featureName: BUG-023-002: Health probe HTTP-GET duplication (checkMLSidecar / checkOllama)

executionHistory.evidence: Extracted shared bounded-GET probe skeleton from checkMLSidecar (29 lines) and checkOllama (28 lines) into a single new probeHTTPGet(ctx, url, client) bool helper in internal/api/health.go. Both wrappers are now thin status translators (10 and 9 lines respectively): empty-URL guard returns ServiceStatus{Status:"not_configured"}, helper-false returns ServiceStatus{Status:"down"}, helper-true returns ServiceStatus{Status:"up"} (ML adds ModelLoaded:&loaded). No signature changes, no new package state, mlClient sync.Once pathway untouched. Single file modified.

All 8 pre-existing wrapper tests PASS: TestCheckMLSidecar_EmptyURL, TestCheckMLSidecar_HealthyResponse, TestCheckMLSidecar_UnhealthyResponse, TestCheckMLSidecar_ConnectionRefused, TestCheckOllama_Healthy, TestCheckOllama_Down, TestCheckOllama_NotConfigured, TestCheckOllama_Unreachable. Race-detector regression for SCN-023-01 (TestMLClient_ConcurrentAccess) PASS under -race. `go build ./...` exit 0. Raw output captured in report.md > Test Evidence.

Behavioural envelope preserved across 4 branches per wrapper (empty URL -> not_configured; transport error -> down; non-200 -> down; 200 -> up). Existing tests serve as the regression suite (they exercise all 4 branches per wrapper). Boundary check: only internal/api/health.go is modified in production code; all other touches confined to the new bug folder. Parent spec 023 stays certified done (no parent artifact change required).

Artifact lint pending invocation at the bug folder.
